title: STAGE (STop And Go Extraction) C18 Tips for Desalting and Clean Up
author: Teesha C Luehr
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.y9pfz5n
materials: Hydrate the tip with 100 µL, Buffer A

steps.title: Abbreviations
steps.instruction: Abbreviations: ACN: acetonitrile TFA: trifluoroacetic acid FA: Formic Acid MeOH: Methanol

steps.title: Buffer A
steps.instruction: Buffer A: 2% ACN with 0.1% TFA To make 100 mL 10 mL, 1% TFA 2 mL, ACN 88 mL, Milli-Q water

steps.title: Buffer B
steps.instruction: Buffer B: Anywhere from 30-80% ACN with 0.1% TFA General peptides are eluted with 60% ACN with 0.1% TFA (***this is also used for tip conditioning***) To make 100 mL 10 mL, 1% TFA 60 mL, ACN 30 mL, Milli-Q water Immunopeptides are eluted with 30% with 0.1% TFA To make 100 mL 10 mL, 1% TFA 30 mL, ACN 60 mL, Milli-Q water

steps.title: Mass Spectrometry Buffer A
steps.instruction: Mass Spectrometry Buffer A: 2% ACN with 0.1% FA It is important to use FORMIC ACID for mass spectrometry solvents

steps.title: Prepare as many tips as necessary...
steps.instruction: Prepare as many tips as necessary by one of two methods: C18 empore filter or frit + C18 powder

steps.title: The filter/frit will act to keep...
steps.instruction: The filter/frit will act to keep the powder in the P200 pipette tip. The frit has a pore size, but this is much larger than the pore size of C18 filter.

steps.title: Punch out a disk using a 17G flat-tipped syringe
steps.instruction: Punch out a disk using a 17G flat-tipped syringe. Use C18 Empore filters or a SPE frit. Careful not to squish the frit as this will cause a blockage The easiest way to do this is to line the syringe up gently on top of the frit, then hammer the syringe down (rather than twisting and manually pushing).

steps.title: Eject the frit/frit disk into a...
steps.instruction: Eject the frit/frit disk into a P200 pipette tip with an unfolded paper clip

steps.title: Ensure that the filter/frit is secrely...
steps.instruction: Ensure that the filter/frit is secrely wedged in the bottom of the tip. The frits are much harder than the C18 filter, therefore, more resistant to folding at the bottom of the tip.

steps.title: Using a multi-channel pipette resevoir, add...
steps.instruction: Using a multi-channel pipette resevoir, add in C18 powder and wet it with methanol to create a slurry This step is optional if a C18 membrane was used.

steps.title: Using a pipette, add C18 slurry...
steps.instruction: Using a pipette, add C18 slurry on top of the filter/frit

steps.title: Compact the slurry and remove the...
steps.instruction: Compact the slurry and remove the ethanol by forcing the methanol from the slurry. This can be done with a Luer lock syringe fitted to the tip of the pipette tip. Alternatively, this can be done by centrifuging the tips at 800g. However, when conditioning the tips it is advised to manually force the solvent through to check the tightness/loseness of the frit + slurry.

steps.title: If your sample is dry, reconstitue it in Buffer A
steps.instruction: If your sample is dry, reconstitue it in Buffer A. If the sample is aqueous, acidify it with 1% TFA to a pH <2.5. Reconsitute in 20 µL, Buffer A or up to 100 µL, Buffer A depending on the sample concentration. It is important to acheive a pH less than 2.5 to ensure all amino acids are in their protonated form

steps.title: If the concentration of the sample...
steps.instruction: If the concentration of the sample is unknown, determine it by NanoDrop 10mg of C18 powder can handle ~ 200-500µg of protein from a full cell lysate

steps.title: Condition the tip with 100 µL,...
steps.instruction: Condition the tip with 100 µL, Buffer B (50-60% ACN with 0.1% TFA)

steps.title: Wash the tip with 200 µL, 1% TFA Optional
steps.instruction: Wash the tip with 200 µL, 1% TFA Optional: collect flow through and store at -20˚C in case of improper tip usage Optional: If there is not time to elute and dry the sample, the peptides can be stored "dry" on the tip at 4˚C overnight or -20˚C for longer periods. This is a better option than eluting the peptides and storing in Buffer B.

steps.title: Elute the sample with 2X 100...
steps.instruction: Elute the sample with 2X 100 µL, Buffer B (your selected % ACN) into a 0.5 mL, microtube or a 96-well plate

steps.title: Pass the reconstituted sample slowly through...
steps.instruction: Pass the reconstituted sample slowly through the tip Optional: collect flow through and store at -20˚C in case of improper tip usage

steps.title: Dry the sample completely in a...
steps.instruction: Dry the sample completely in a SpeedVac and recostitute in the Mass Spec Buffer

steps.title: A different amount of C18 powder...
steps.instruction: A different amount of C18 powder will be added each time as the slurry is not homogenous. Add slurry up to a desired height. 10mg of C18 powder is ~11mm above the frit.
